quiz.title: Unit 10 Quiz 3, Grade 11 Chemistry
quiz.subtitle: Factors Affecting Dissolution Rate and Molarity Calculations
quiz.subject: Chemistry
quiz.topic: Factors Affecting Dissolution Rate, Surface Area, Temperature, Stirring, Saturation Types, Molarity Calculations

questions.question: Which would NOT increase the rate at which a sugar cube dissolves?
questions.explanation: Reducing the amount of solvent decreases the rate of dissolution because there is less solvent available to interact with the solute. Crushing increases surface area, stirring increases contact, and heating increases molecular motion — all increase dissolution rate.

questions.question: You can make a solute dissolve more quickly in a solvent by:
questions.explanation: Heating the solvent increases molecular motion and collision frequency, speeding up dissolution. Adding more solute (A) does not increase rate, adding ice (B) cools the solvent, and removing solvent (D) reduces the amount available.

questions.question: A solution that contains all of the solute it can hold at a given temperature is:
questions.explanation: A saturated solution contains the maximum amount of solute that can dissolve at that temperature. Unsaturated has less, supersaturated has more than normally possible.

questions.question: The amount of solute that can be dissolved in a specific amount of solvent at a given temperature is its:
questions.explanation: Solubility is the maximum amount of solute that can dissolve in a given amount of solvent at a specific temperature. Concentration is the amount actually dissolved.

questions.question: Why does granulated sugar dissolve faster in tea than a solid sugar cube of the same mass?
options: Granulated sugar has a much larger surface area exposed to the tea
Granulated sugar contains more sucrose molecules per gram than a sugar cube
Sugar cubes are chemically bonded more strongly than granulated sugar
Granulated sugar increases the temperature of the tea, which speeds up dissolution
questions.explanation: Granulated sugar has many small crystals with much greater total surface area than a single cube. More surface area means more contact with solvent, leading to faster dissolution.

questions.question: If you are trying to dissolve salt in water as quickly as possible, should you use a whisk or let it sit still?
options: Use a whisk, because stirring increases contact between salt particles and water
Let it sit still, because motion interferes with the natural diffusion of salt ions
Let it sit still, because stirring lowers the temperature of the water and slows dissolving
Use a whisk only if the salt is finely ground; stirring has no effect on larger crystals
questions.explanation: Stirring brings fresh solvent into contact with the solute surface and prevents the buildup of a saturated layer around the particles, significantly increasing dissolution rate.

questions.question: Why does a solute dissolve faster in boiling water than in ice-cold water?
options: Water molecules move faster and interact more with the solute
Heat causes a chemical change and acts as a catalyst
Cold water traps solute particles in place, slowing the process
Solutes cannot dissolve at low temperatures
questions.explanation: Higher temperature increases the kinetic energy of water molecules, causing them to move faster and collide more frequently with solute particles, speeding up dissolution.

questions.question: Why does 'swirling' a flask help a solid disappear into a liquid?
options: It increases contact between solute and solvent
It breaks the chemical bonds binding the liquid
It breaks the chemical bonds binding the solid
It causes more ions to precipitate out of solution
questions.explanation: Swirling increases contact between the solute and solvent, bringing fresh solvent molecules to the solid surface and removing the saturated layer that forms around the solid. This speeds up dissolution without breaking chemical bonds.

questions.question: Which of the following would most likely undergo dissociation when dissolved in water?
questions.explanation: Sodium bromide is an ionic compound that dissociates into Na⁺ and Br⁻ ions when dissolved in water. The other substances are molecular and do not dissociate.

questions.question: Calculate the molarity of a solution that contains 4.50 moles of solute in 750 mL of solution.
questions.explanation:
Volume = 750 mL = 0.750 L
Molarity = moles / volume (L) = 4.50 / 0.750 = 6.00 M

questions.question: Determine the molarity of a solution prepared by dissolving 0.85 moles of a substance in 2.00 L of solution.
What is the molarity of a solution that has 6.25 moles of solute in 1250 mL of solution?
Calculate the molarity of a solution containing 1.20 moles of solute in 400 mL of solution.
Find the molarity of a solution that contains 9.00 moles of solute in 3.60 L of solution.
Determine the molarity of a solution with 0.375 moles of solute dissolved in 500 mL of solution.

scoreMessages.90: Excellent! You've mastered dissolution factors and molarity calculations! 🧪📊
scoreMessages.70: Good job! Review the factors that affect dissolution rate and molarity formula.
scoreMessages.50: Satisfactory. Focus on converting mL to L correctly in molarity problems.
scoreMessages.0: Please review factors affecting dissolution and molarity calculations (M = moles/L).
